response.overview: Forty-Five Minutes From Broadway is a three-act musical by George M. Cohan written about the town of New Rochelle, New York.[1] The title refers to the 45-minute train ride from New Rochelle to Broadway.[2] The story concerns a romance between a maid who stands to inherit a fortune and an unpopular out-of-towner.  The musical premiered on Broadway in 1906 and was briefly revived in 1912. It was adapted as silent film and broadcast live on television in 1959.
response.title: Forty-five Minutes from Broadway

results.name: Forty-five Minutes from Broadway (1/4) (1906 George M. Cohan) | Omnibus With Alistair Cooke
